Sunday 12 July 2026 · 11am–4pm · Free Entry

Gledhow Valley
Green Fair

A free, family-friendly community fair celebrating Gledhow Valley Woods and the people who make this place special. Gledhow Valley Road will be car-free for the day.

Volunteer with us How to get there Follow on Facebook
📅
Date
Sunday 12 July 2026
🕑
Time
11am – 4pm
📍
Location
Gledhow Valley Road, Leeds
🎟️
Entry
Free — all welcome

About the Green Fair

The Gledhow Valley Green Fair is an annual community celebration of Gledhow Valley Woods and the wonderful neighbourhood it runs through. The fair brings together local organisations, artists, producers, and residents for a day of activities, entertainment, food, and exploration.

Gledhow Valley Road is closed to motor vehicles for the day, creating a safe, relaxed space where the whole community can come together. The fair is free to enter and suitable for all ages — from young families to seasoned woodland-walkers.

The fair is organised by a volunteer committee with support from the Friends of Gledhow Valley Woods. All proceeds help support conservation and community activities in the valley.

How to get there

Gledhow Valley Road will be car-free on the day of the fair. Please travel by bus, bike, or on foot — and help keep the valley car-free!

🚌 Via Roundhay Road (east end)

Routes 12, 13, 13A, 874, X98, X99

🚌 Via Harrogate Road (west end)

Routes 2, 3, 3A, 91, 36

🚲 By bike

Cycle parking will be available at the fair. The valley road provides a pleasant approach from several directions.

🗺️
Map: Gledhow Valley Road location — map embed placeholder

Gledhow Valley Road, Leeds, LS8 / LS17

Volunteer with us

The Green Fair is made possible entirely by volunteers. We need help with set-up and pack-down, stewarding, and managing activities on the day. If you'd like to get involved, please get in touch.

Volunteer signup — coming soon

A volunteer signup form will be available here closer to the fair date. In the meantime, contact us to register your interest.

Sponsors

The Gledhow Valley Green Fair is supported by local businesses and community organisations. Interested in sponsoring the 2026 fair? Get in touch.

2026 sponsors — to be announced. Contact us to find out about sponsorship opportunities.

Frequently asked questions

How do I get to the Green Fair? ▾

The fair is on Gledhow Valley Road, which will be car-free on the day. We strongly encourage you to travel by public transport, bicycle, or on foot.

By bus:

  • Routes 12, 13, 13A, 874, X98, X99 stop on Roundhay Road (east end)
  • Routes 2, 3, 3A, 91, 36 stop on Harrogate Road (west end)

Both ends of Gledhow Valley Road are a short walk from bus stops on these routes.

Is the Green Fair free to attend? ▾

Yes — entry to the Gledhow Valley Green Fair is completely free. There is no ticket required and no charge to enter.

Some stalls and activities may charge individually, but the fair itself is free and open to everyone.

Can I park near the fair? ▾

Gledhow Valley Road is car-free on the day of the fair, so there is no parking on the road itself.

Limited residential parking is available in the surrounding streets, but please be considerate of local residents and use public transport where possible. Bus routes serving the area include 12, 13, 13A, 874, X98, X99 (via Roundhay Road) and 2, 3, 3A, 91, 36 (via Harrogate Road).

Can I bring my dog? ▾

Well-behaved dogs on leads are welcome at the Green Fair. Please clean up after your dog.

Friends of Gledhow Valley Woods is a registered charity protecting and enhancing Gledhow Valley Woods in north Leeds since 1996.

Registered Charity No. 1169599
